title: TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined
solution_steps: 1. Use optional chaining: `obj?.prop?.innerProp`
2. Use nullish coalescing: `const val = obj?.prop ?? 'default'`
3. Add null/undefined checks: `if (obj?.prop)`
4. Initialize variables before use
5. Check API response shape matches expected structure
6. Use TypeScript for compile-time null safety

title: React: setState is asynchronous
solution_steps: 1. `setState` is async; don't read `this.state` immediately after setState
2. Use functional updater: `setCount(prev => prev + 1)`
3. Use `useEffect` to react to state changes
4. Multiple setState calls are batched in React 18
5. For class components: pass callback as second arg to `setState`

title: JavaScript: async/await try/catch pattern
solution_steps: 1. Wrap await calls in try/catch blocks
2. Use a wrapper function for consistent error handling
3. Add .catch() to promises if not using await
4. Use Promise.allSettled() instead of Promise.all() for fault tolerance
5. Add global error handlers: `window.onunhandledrejection`

title: Node.js: module.exports vs exports confusion
solution_steps: 1. `module.exports` is the actual export object
2. `exports` is a shorthand alias for `module.exports`
3. Reassigning `exports` breaks the reference to `module.exports`
4. Use `module.exports = { ... }` for exporting an object
5. Use `exports.funcName = ...` to add properties safely

title: TypeScript: strict null checks errors
solution_steps: 1. Add null checks: `if (value !== null && value !== undefined)`
2. Use optional chaining: `value?.property`
3. Use non-null assertion: `value!.property` (use sparingly)
4. Use type guards: `typeof value === 'string'`
5. Use definite assignment assertion: `let x!: number`
6. Configure `strictNullChecks: true` in tsconfig.json

title: React: useEffect missing dependencies
error_patterns: React Hook useEffect has missing dependencies
solution_steps: 1. Include all variables used inside useEffect in dependency array
2. Use `useCallback` for functions passed as deps
3. Use `useRef` for values that shouldn't trigger re-runs
4. Install `eslint-plugin-react-hooks` for automatic linting
5. Split effects if deps are unrelated
6. Consider if you actually need useEffect (derived state)

title: JavaScript: 'this' context loss in callbacks
solution_steps: 1. Use arrow functions to preserve lexical `this`
2. Use `.bind(this)` on regular functions
3. Store `this` in a variable: `const self = this;` (pre-ES6)
4. Use class property arrow functions for class methods
5. In React event handlers, use arrow functions or bind in constructor

title: Node.js: Error-first callbacks (callback hell)
solution_steps: 1. Convert callbacks to promises with `util.promisify()`
2. Use async/await instead of nested callbacks
3. If using callbacks, always check `err` parameter first
4. Use `async` libraries (caolan/async) for control flow
5. Refactor deeply nested callbacks into named functions

title: React: keys in lists
error_patterns: Warning: Each child in a list should have a unique "key" prop
solution_steps: 1. Use stable IDs from data as keys: `key={item.id}`
2. Avoid using index as key (causes re-render issues on reorder)
3. Keys must be unique among siblings
4. Keys help React identify which items changed
5. Use `crypto.randomUUID()` or `nanoid` if no natural ID exists

title: JavaScript: typeof vs instanceof
solution_steps: 1. Use `typeof` for primitives: string, number, boolean, undefined, symbol, bigint
2. Use `Array.isArray()` for arrays (typeof returns 'object')
3. Use `instanceof` for class instances
4. Use `Object.prototype.toString.call(obj)` for precise type
5. Use `===` to compare to null/undefined

title: Node.js: process.env not loading .env file
solution_steps: 1. Install: `npm install dotenv`
2. Call `require('dotenv').config()` as early as possible
3. Ensure .env file is in the root directory (same as entry point)
4. Use explicit path: `dotenv.config({ path: '../.env' })`
5. .env must not have quotes around values
6. Restart the process after modifying .env

title: React: unmounted component state update
error_patterns: Can't perform a React state update on an unmounted component
Warning: Can't perform a React state update
solution_steps: 1. Use a cleanup function in useEffect to cancel async operations
2. Use `AbortController` for fetch requests
3. Track mounted state with a ref: `const isMounted = useRef(true)`
4. Cancel subscriptions in useEffect cleanup
5. Use `useCallback` to prevent unnecessary re-renders

title: JavaScript: Floating point precision in JS
solution_steps: 1. Use `toFixed(n)` for display (returns string)
2. Use `Math.round(num * 100) / 100` for 2 decimal rounding
3. Multiply to integer for calculations
4. Use libraries like `decimal.js` or `bignumber.js`
5. Be aware of IEEE 754 double precision limits

title: TypeScript: union types and type narrowing
error_patterns: Property does not exist on type
solution_steps: 1. Use type guards: `typeof`, `instanceof`, `in`, custom guards
2. Use discriminated unions with `type` field
3. Use `asserts` keyword for assertion functions
4. Use `is` keyword for custom type predicates
5. Use exhaustive switch with `never` for completeness checking

title: Node.js: npm install ENOENT / permissions
error_patterns: ENOENT: no such file or directory
solution_steps: 1. Delete `node_modules` and `package-lock.json` and retry
2. Run: `npm cache clean --force`
3. On Windows: close VS Code/IDE and run terminal as Admin
4. Check for corporate proxy: `npm config set proxy http://proxy:port`
5. Use Node Version Manager to switch Node versions
6. Disable antivirus temporarily
code_example:
rm -rf node_modules package-lock.json
npm cache clean --force
npm install

title: React: useContext not re-rendering
solution_steps: 1. Ensure Provider value is not a new object each render: `useMemo()`
2. Split contexts if unrelated values change together
3. Check that the consumer is inside the Provider tree
4. Use `useMemo` for context value reference stability
5. Consider using `useReducer` for complex state in context

title: JavaScript: spread operator shallow copy
solution_steps: 1. `{...obj}` and `[...arr]` are SHALLOW copies
2. For nested objects, spread each level: `{...obj, nested: {...obj.nested}}`
3. Use `structuredClone()` for deep clone (modern browsers/Node 17+)
4. Use `JSON.parse(JSON.stringify(obj))` for simple deep clone (loses functions, Date, undefined)
5. Use `immer` or `lodash.cloneDeep` for complex immutable updates

title: Node.js: EventEmitter memory leak
solution_steps: 1. Remove listeners that are no longer needed: `.removeListener()`
2. Use `emitter.once()` instead of `emitter.on()` for one-time events
3. Increase max listeners: `emitter.setMaxListeners(20)` (temporary)
4. Use `emitter.rawListeners()` to inspect attached listeners
5. Track listener count with `emitter.listenerCount()`

title: React: useCallback and useMemo optimization
solution_steps: 1. `useCallback(fn, deps)` memoizes function reference
2. `useMemo(() => value, deps)` memoizes computed value
3. Only use when passing to child components wrapped in `React.memo`
4. Don't optimize prematurely — measure first with Profiler
5. Include all dependencies or you'll get stale values

title: JavaScript: closures in loops
solution_steps: 1. Use `let` instead of `var` in for loops (block scoping)
2. Use IIFE (Immediately Invoked Function Expression): `(function(i) { ... })(i)`
3. Use `.forEach()` which creates a new scope per iteration
4. Use `for...of` which uses block scoping per iteration
5. Use `let` in the for initialization: `for (let i = 0; ...)`

title: Node.js: stream backpressure handling
solution_steps: 1. Use `stream.pipeline()` instead of `.pipe()` for error handling
2. Listen to `drain` event before writing more data
3. Use `highWaterMark` option to control buffer size
4. Use `.write()` return value to know if buffer is full
5. Use Transform streams for processing with backpressure support

title: React: controlled vs uncontrolled components
error_patterns: A component is changing an uncontrolled input to be controlled
changing a controlled input to be uncontrolled
solution_steps: 1. Controlled: state manages value, use `value` + `onChange`
2. Uncontrolled: DOM manages value, use `defaultValue` + `ref`
3. Don't switch between controlled and uncontrolled — stay consistent
4. Use `key` prop to re-mount component to reset state
5. For forms: use `react-hook-form` for complex validation

title: JavaScript: debounce and throttle
solution_steps: 1. Debounce: wait until activity stops (search input, resize)
2. Throttle: limit rate (scroll, mousemove)
3. Use `lodash.debounce` or `lodash.throttle`
4. Use `AbortController` to cancel pending requests on new debounce
5. Use React `use-debounce` hook for debounced state

title: TypeScript: using `any` vs `unknown`
solution_steps: 1. Prefer `unknown` over `any` for incoming data (API, user input)
2. `unknown` requires type narrowing before use
3. `any` disables all type checking (use as last resort)
4. Use type guards to narrow `unknown`: `typeof`, `instanceof`, custom
5. For parsing, use Zod or io-ts for runtime validation

title: Node.js: Express middleware error handling
solution_steps: 1. Error-handling middleware has 4 parameters: `(err, req, res, next)`
2. Wrap async route handlers: `asyncHandler(fn)` or use express-async-errors
3. Always call `next(err)` for async errors
4. Define error middleware last in the chain
5. Don't send headers twice — check `res.headersSent`

title: JavaScript: === vs ==
solution_steps: 1. Always use `===` (strict equality) unless you explicitly need type coercion
2. `==` performs type coercion (e.g., 0 == '' is true)
3. `===` checks both value and type
4. Exception: `== null` checks both null and undefined
5. Use `Object.is()` for NaN/-0 comparisons
code_example:
console.log('' == false);    // true (coerced)
console.log('' === false);   // false
console.log(null == undefined);  // true
console.log(Object.is(NaN, NaN));  // true

title: React: prop drilling vs composition
solution_steps: 1. Use composition: pass components as children/props
2. Use Context for shared state (theme, auth, locale)
3. Extract `Compound Components` pattern
4. Use state management libraries (Zustand, Jotai, Redux) for complex state
5. Consider if the intermediate component needs the prop
code_example:
// Composition instead of drilling
<Page>
  <Page.Header>
    <Title text="Hello" />
  </Page.Header>
  <Page.Body>
    {children}
  </Page.Body>
</Page>

title: JavaScript: Promise.all vs Promise.allSettled
solution_steps: 1. `Promise.all` rejects fast on first rejection (all-or-nothing)
2. `Promise.allSettled` waits for all, returns { status, value/reason }
3. Use `Promise.allSettled` when you need partial results
4. Use `Promise.any` for first fulfilled promise
5. Use `Promise.race` for first settled (fulfilled or rejected)

title: Node.js: module caching and singleton
solution_steps: 1. Node.js caches modules after first `require` (singleton by default)
2. To invalidate: `delete require.cache[require.resolve('./module')]`
3. Module-level state is shared across the process
4. Use factory functions to create fresh instances
5. For tests: use proxyquire or rewire for module mocking

title: JavaScript: Date object pitfalls
solution_steps: 1. `getMonth()` is 0-indexed (0 = January), `getDate()` is 1-indexed
2. Use libraries: `date-fns`, `luxon`, or `dayjs`
3. For ISO strings: `new Date('2024-01-01T00:00:00Z')` (Z = UTC)
4. Use `.toISOString()` for consistent serialization
5. Use `Intl.DateTimeFormat` for locale-aware formatting

title: React: useReducer for complex state
solution_steps: 1. Use `useReducer` when state logic is complex or depends on previous state
2. Reducer must be pure function: (state, action) => newState
3. Dispatch actions with type and payload: `{ type: 'INCREMENT', payload: 5 }`
4. Use `useReducer` + Context for global state without Redux
5. Use `immer` with useReducer for immutable updates

title: JavaScript: module import/export named vs default
solution_steps: 1. Named exports: `export const foo = ...` → `import { foo } from './m'`
2. Default export: `export default Foo` → `import Foo from './m'`
3. Mix both: `import Foo, { bar, baz } from './m'`
4. Re-export: `export { foo } from './other'`
5. Use `as` for aliasing: `import { foo as bar } from './m'`
